title: Grid Layout Options
description: Configuration options for the grid layout system. This interface defines all the customizable properties that control the behavior, appearance, and functionality of the grid layout container.

props.name: column
props.description: Number of columns in the grid. For nested grids, use 'auto' to match the container grid-item current width. Default is 12.

props.name: disableDrag
props.description: Disallows dragging of widgets. When true, users cannot drag widgets to new positions.

props.name: disableResize
props.description: Disallows resizing of widgets. When true, users cannot resize widgets by dragging their edges.

props.name: gap
props.description: Gap between grid item and content. Can be an integer (px), string with units (ex: '2em', '20px'), or space-separated values for all 4 sides (ex: '5px 10px 0 20px').

props.name: cellHeight
props.description: Height of one cell. Can be an integer (px), string with units, 0 (no styles generated), 'auto' (calculated square cells), or 'initial' (square cells that don't resize).

props.name: rtl
props.description: Enables right-to-left layout. Can be true, false, or 'auto' for automatic detection.

props.name: maxRow
props.description: Maximum number of rows allowed in the grid. 0 means no maximum limit.

props.name: minRow
props.description: Minimum number of rows in the grid. Can also be set using min-height CSS attribute on the grid div.

props.name: row
props.description: Fixed number of rows. This is a shortcut for setting both minRow and maxRow to the same value. 0 means no constraint.

props.name: removableSelector
props.description: CSS selector string (ex: '.trash') where widgets can be dropped to remove them. When set, widgets will be removed by dropping them on this element.

props.name: removable
props.description: Enables the option to remove widgets from the grid.

props.name: acceptWidgets
props.description: Allows widgets to be dragged from other grids or from outside sources into this grid.

props.name: float
props.description: Enables floating widgets that can overlap and move freely without snapping to grid positions.

props.name: dragHandlerSelector
props.description: CSS selector for the draggable handle element. Only this element will be draggable, not the entire widget.

props.name: responsiveLayout
props.description: Array of responsive breakpoints defining column layout behavior at different screen widths. Each object specifies a width threshold and corresponding column count.
